To determine \( f(2015, 2) \), we first observe the recursive behavior of the function \( f(i, j) \):

1. **Base Case (\( i = 0 \))**: \( f(0, j) = \text{mod}_5(j + 1) \), which cycles through \( 1, 2, 3, 4, 0 \) as \( j \) increases from \( 0 \) to \( 4 \).
2. **Recursive Step for \( j = 0 \)**: \( f(i, 0) = f(i-1, 1) \), effectively shifting the input \( j \) to \( 1 \) for the next iteration.
3. **Recursive Step for \( 1 \leq j \leq 4 \)**: \( f(i, j) = f(i-1, f(i, j-1)) \), which builds a nested dependency
